title: hieracium umbellatum var. subhirsutissimum
intro: Hieracium umbellatum var. subhirsutissimum, also known as False Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family, characterized by its hairy stems, narrow leaves, and yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hieracium umbellatum var. subhirsutissimum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
